artifacts.content:
From: emily.carter84@gmail.com
To: jenna.morris@outlook.com
Date: 2025-07-22 10:14 PM
Subject: I think I need to slow down

Hey Jen,

Kids are finally in bed, dishwasher is humming, and I’m sitting here with (surprise) a glass of Pinot and my laptop, so of course my brain decided now is the time to spiral.

I know I joked back in the spring about my “two glasses most nights” habit, but it’s starting to feel less funny and more like a coping mechanism I automatically reach for after bedtime. Tonight was one of those particularly stressful nights — Ethan melted down over his dinosaur pajamas being in the wash, Olivia had a full-on tears situation about her art club project, and I still hadn’t answered half the emails from Riverfront.

Ever since Dad’s stroke at the end of 2023 and then the weird memory stuff that started around mid-2024, it’s like my baseline anxiety never came back down. I keep flashing back to when he got disoriented walking around our neighborhood here in Richmond last fall and couldn’t remember which way Willow Creek Drive was. Remember that email Rebecca sent in September 2025 with the subject line “Do we need to talk about early dementia for Dad?” I still have it starred. It feels like we’re all waiting for the other shoe to drop.

Work isn’t helping. Mark is still doing his “Need this tonight, no excuses” thing at 10:45 PM and making little digs about my “mom priorities” in front of the team. I finally went to HR in March about it — just an informal complaint, but still — and ever since then I’ve been hyper-aware of every personal call, every time I leave at 5:00 on the dot. No wonder my jaw is permanently clenched.

So yeah, glass of wine number two has become my nightly “off switch.” I’m not blacking out or anything, but I don’t love that I’m relying on it. Between the stress drinking, Dad’s possible early dementia, and the HR mess with Mark, my body is basically a ball of cortisol.

I did finally start seeing that therapist I told you about — Dr. Karen Mitchell at James River Counseling Center — every other Wednesday at 5:30. She’s nice, but I still feel weird saying out loud that I’m not coping great.

Anyway, this is long. Just needed to say it to someone who won’t judge me. Tell me about something silly your kids did this week so I can think about anything else.

Love,
Em

metadata.subject: I think I need to slow down

artifacts.content:
From: emily.carter84@gmail.com
To: rebecca.thompson@gmail.com
Date: 2025-09-19 6:58 AM
Subject: Re: Do we need to talk about early dementia for Dad?

Hey Bec,

I read your email twice last night after we got the kids down and then again this morning with coffee. The subject line alone — “Do we need to talk about early dementia for Dad?” — made my stomach drop, even though it’s not like we haven’t both been thinking it since mid-2024.

I keep going back to that weekend last October when you all were here and Dad got turned around walking back from the little park off Grove to our place on Willow Creek Drive. It’s literally a straight shot and he still called me from two blocks away sounding panicked. At the time I blamed it on stroke recovery and being tired, but now, with the repeating questions and him forgetting that Olivia’s in fourth grade this year, it’s harder to brush off.

I do think we should push for a neurology consult at Mary Washington in Fredericksburg. Maybe start with his primary and ask for a referral? Mom will freak out about the word “dementia,” so maybe we frame it as a “memory checkup” because of the late-2023 stroke. I can come up one Friday if you want backup. We can tag team like we did when we convinced them to finally downsize last year.

On my end, the timing is rough. Work at Riverfront is intense — Mark’s still being Mark, and things have been tense since I went to HR in March about the late-night “Need this tonight, no excuses” emails. I’m also trying to keep my own stuff under control. My blood pressure’s been borderline since August 2023, and VCU Health has me tracking readings in that “Emily BP readings – do not share” Google Sheet. I have a med adjustment appointment 3/18/25, so I really don’t need more stress, but here we are.

I’ve been talking through a lot of this with Dr. Karen Mitchell at James River Counseling Center (every other Wednesday at 5:30). She keeps reminding me we can’t control everything, just how we respond, which sounds very Pinterest-quote but is also…not wrong.

Call me tonight after the kids are in bed? Maybe around 8:30?

Love you,
Em

metadata.subject: Re: Do we need to talk about early dementia for Dad?

artifacts.content:
Title: Health + stress notes – don’t forget
Date: 2025-11-05 9:12 PM

- Blood pressure
  - Keep updating “Emily BP readings – do not share” Google Sheet at least 3x/week.
  - Bring printed copy or iPad to next VCU Health Adult Primary Care visit.
  - Ask Dr. Shah at the 3/18/25 med adjustment appointment whether the spikes on days after big Riverfront deadlines are concerning or just normal stress.

- Teeth / dental
  - November 2025: cracked molar on upper left (thanks, stress). Richmond Family Dental on Grove Ave, Dr. Alan Pierce.
  - He said nighttime grinding is likely from “high work and caregiver stress” (in my chart, ugh).
  - Night guard ordered; remember to actually WEAR it so we don’t repeat the minor but embarrassing procedure.

- Mental health
  - Therapy with Dr. Karen Mitchell at James River Counseling Center every other Wednesday at 5:30 PM.
  - Block off calendar as “Therapy – Dr. Mitchell (please handle kids’ pickup)” so Michael remembers bus stop and dinner.
  - Note: Avoid scheduling work calls after 5 on those days; I’m useless afterward and it defeats the purpose.

- Communication preferences (for me to tell offices)
  - Cell (804-555-2398) between 8 AM–7 PM for urgent things or same-day changes.
  - Email (emily.carter84@gmail.com) for lab results, BP instructions, etc., so I can add to spreadsheet.
  - Home phone only for generic reminders.
  - Ask them NOT to leave detailed medical info on voicemail — after the cracked tooth last November I realized I don’t want the kids or Michael overhearing sensitive stuff.

- Bigger picture
  - Remember: Dad’s stroke (late 2023) + his possible early dementia + work/HR drama with Mark are all huge stressors. My body is not just “overreacting.”
  - Goal for 2026: more walks, fewer “two glasses most nights” evenings alone on the couch.

From: emily.carter84@gmail.com
To: michael.carter@atlanticunion.com
Date: 2025-03-11 2:03 PM
Subject: Wednesday coverage + money stuff

Hey love,

Popping this off between meetings so I don’t forget.

1) Tomorrow (Wed) evening
I have my standing therapy appointment with Dr. Karen Mitchell at James River Counseling Center at 5:30 PM. Can you handle both kids’ pickup and dinner? I put a hold on the shared calendar as “Therapy – Dr. Mitchell (please handle kids’ pickup)” but I know your Outlook gets crazy.

Plan:
- Olivia off the bus at 3:20 at the corner by Willow Creek.
- Ethan from after-care by 5:15.
- Tacos stuff is in the fridge; worst case, there’s always waffles.

2) Money
I got a reminder that the Money Management International draft hits on the 15th like always. Since we enrolled in that debt management program after we downsized to Willow Creek in June 2023, it’s actually been a relief to have the $18,000 in credit card/medical mess consolidated, but it does mean we have to be careful the joint account at Atlantic Union has enough in it.

Can you double-check the balance before Friday? I’m still a little twitchy after the whole Chase card situation — finding out in October 2024 that there was about $9,000 on a card I didn’t even know existed was…not our finest marriage moment. I know you’ve been great about it since, and the online electronics/gaming splurges have stopped, but my brain still goes to worst-case.

Also, Richmond Family Dental just billed for the cracked molar fix, so that’ll hit next week. Between that and the MMI draft, I’d rather know now if we need to move anything around.

3) Work
Mark dropped another “Need this tonight, no excuses” email at 11:30 PM yesterday. I’m trying not to read too much into it after the HR conversation in March, but it still makes me feel like I can’t ever fully unplug. All the more reason I need tomorrow’s session.

Text me if you see any red flags in the account. Otherwise, see you tonight around 6.

E

Event: Therapy – Dr. Mitchell (please handle kids’ pickup)
Date: Wednesday, 2025-12-17
Time: 5:30 PM – 6:30 PM
Location: James River Counseling Center, Richmond
Attendees: Emily

Description/Notes:

Standing every-other-Wednesday therapy session with Dr. Karen Mitchell at James River Counseling Center. Started going in February 2024 when the anxiety about juggling Riverfront deadlines, the kids’ schedules, and Dad’s stroke recovery finally caught up with me.

To remember:
- Leave the office by 4:45 PM to beat traffic from downtown to the counseling center.
- Michael to handle both kids that night: Olivia off the bus at 3:20 on Willow Creek Drive, Ethan from after-care by 5:15.
- Quick dinner ideas: sheet-pan chicken and veggies, or leftovers.

Context (for me):
- Dad is still recovering from the late-2023 stroke and the mild cognitive decline we’ve noticed since mid-2024 (forgetting conversations, getting turned around near our apartment). Rebecca and I literally have an email thread titled “Do we need to talk about early dementia for Dad?” from September 2025.
- Work remains tense with Mark after the informal HR complaint I filed in March 2025 about his late-night “Need this tonight, no excuses” emails and comments about my “mom priorities.”
- Blood pressure has been borderline since August 2023; monitoring at home and logging in the “Emily BP readings – do not share” spreadsheet.
- November 2025 cracked molar at Richmond Family Dental (Dr. Alan Pierce) was a wake-up call about my stress and jaw clenching.

Goals for this session:
- Talk to Dr. Mitchell about cutting back on the “two glasses most nights” wine habit.
- Figure out how to set firmer work boundaries without tanking my chances at a promotion.
- Process the latest conversation with Mom about taking Dad to Mary Washington Hospital in Fredericksburg for a memory workup.

metadata.title: Therapy – Dr. Mitchell (please handle kids’ pickup)

artifacts.content:
From: emily.carter84@gmail.com
To: billing@richmondfamilydental.com
Date: 2025-11-29 3:47 PM
Subject: Billing + communication preferences

Hi there,

This is Emily Rose Carter (DOB 04/14/84). I was in earlier this month at Richmond Family Dental on Grove Ave to repair a cracked molar with Dr. Alan Pierce, and to get fitted for a custom night guard.

I had a couple of quick questions about the bill that just came through and also wanted to flag my preferred way to handle future communication.

1) Billing
I see the insurance adjustment, but I’m not 100% clear on the remaining balance for the cracked tooth procedure vs. the night guard. Could you email me a simple breakdown of what’s due now and what will be due when the guard comes in? My email is this address, and that’s the easiest way for me to keep track.

I’m asking partly because my husband and I are on a pretty tight budget. After we downsized to Willow Creek Drive in June 2023, we enrolled in a debt management program with Money Management International downtown to consolidate about $18,000 in credit card and medical debt. They draft automatically from our joint Atlantic Union Bank account on the 15th of each month, so I just need to make sure your charges don’t collide with that.

2) Communication
For anything time-sensitive, please call my cell at (804) 555-2398 between 8:00 AM and 7:00 PM. For non-urgent things (like night guard pickup timing, routine reminders, etc.), email is best.

Please don’t leave detailed medical or billing information on voicemail. After this cracked tooth in November — which Dr. Pierce kindly noted was probably from “high work and caregiver stress” — I realized I’m not comfortable with my kids or my husband overhearing specifics. A short message with a callback number and best time to reach you is perfect.

Thanks so much for your help, and please let me know if you need anything else from me.

Best,
Emily Rose Carter
742 Willow Creek Drive, Apt 3B
Richmond, VA 23221
Cell: 804-555-2398

From: emily.carter84@gmail.com
To: nurseline@vcuhealth.org
Date: 2025-03-10 9:06 AM
Subject: BP monitoring + best way to reach me

Hi,

This is Emily Rose Carter, a patient at VCU Health Adult Primary Care. I have a follow-up/med adjustment appointment scheduled on 3/18/25 regarding my borderline hypertension that was first flagged back in August 2023.

I’ve been keeping a home log of my readings in a Google Sheet labeled “Emily BP readings – do not share” and can bring it to the appointment, but I wanted to ask a couple of quick questions and also clarify the best way to contact me.

1) BP log
Do you prefer that I email the spreadsheet to the nurse ahead of time, or is it fine just to bring it on my phone? I’ve noticed that my numbers are higher on days after big deadlines at Riverfront Creative Group (I’m a Senior Project Coordinator there), and I’m not sure how much detail Dr. Shah wants.

2) Communication
My preferred contact order is:
- Cell: (804) 555-2398 for urgent clinical issues or same-day appointment changes between 8:00 AM and 7:00 PM.
- Email: this address (I check multiple times a day) for non-urgent questions, lab results notifications, and monitoring instructions, especially while we’re still adjusting my blood pressure plan so I can add them to my BP tracking spreadsheet.
- Work phone: (804) 410-6672 only if you absolutely cannot reach me on my cell and it’s about billing or scheduling. Things are a little tense with my boss right now after an HR issue in March, so I’m trying to minimize personal calls at the office.

Please don’t leave detailed medical information on voicemail. A brief message with a callback number and a suggested time to talk is perfect — voicemail is enabled on both my home and cell numbers.

Thanks so much for your help, and please let me know if you need any additional information before the 3/18 appointment.

Best,
Emily Rose Carter
Cell: 804-555-2398
Home: 804-352-7810

metadata.subject: BP monitoring + best way to reach me

artifacts.content:
Hi Michael,

I just wanted to update you on a few things from today, especially since you couldn't join me at the kids' pediatrician appointment. Everything went smoothly, but I had to sign a couple of forms authorizing certain people to make health-related decisions for Olivia and Ethan if we're ever unavailable. As per our discussion, I've added you and Mom (Linda) as authorized individuals. I signed the forms today, so it's effective immediately.

Here's a quick recap:
- **Authorized Person 1**: You, Michael Andrew Carter
  - **Date Added/Removed**: 2025-12-11
- **Authorized Person 2**: Mom, Linda Marie Thompson
  - **Date Added/Removed**: 2025-12-11

Thankfully, there were no issues with the paperwork. I confirmed that nobody refused to sign, and there were no communication barriers or emergencies preventing us from getting everything sorted. The kids are healthy, and the doctor was pleased with their progress.

Also, just a reminder that we should keep an eye on Dad's recovery and possibly plan some time to help out over the holidays. It's important we coordinate with Rebecca and David to make sure everything is covered. Mom will be coming down more frequently, so it would be good to discuss how we can help make her stays more comfortable.

Looking forward to our Saturday night plans. Hopefully, Mom can stay with the kids so we can have a bit of time to ourselves.

Love,
Emily

Signature of Patient Parent or Guardian: Emily R. Carter

metadata.subject: Family Health Updates and Authorization Recap
